title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: New Hampshire officials released a new photo of Rosalie Miller in hopes of solving her 1997 homicide case.
Miller was last seen on December 8, 1996, in Manchester, and her body was found in Auburn on January 20, 1997.
Authorities request information from anyone who interacted with or saw Miller in December 1996, especially near Londonderry Turnpike.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, charged with murder after allegedly killing Javon Lawson, 35, in Cranston, R.I., during a dispute.
Sousa caused a car crash in Swansea, Mass., killing two, after fleeing the scene of the Rhode Island shooting.
Police recovered a pistol and ammunition in Sousa's car, linking the vehicle to the Cranston crime scene.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins considering trading for 26-year-old St. Louis Blues center Robert Thomas before the NHL trade deadline.
Acquiring Thomas would require the Bruins to part with several high-value assets, including draft picks and prospects.
Thomas has signed a contract through the 2030-31 season with a cap hit of $8.125 million per year.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett agreed to a $3,000 fine for allegedly killing trees in Rockport, Maine, without admitting guilt.
The Maine Board of Pesticides Control determined the couple poisoned neighbor Ruth Graham's trees to enhance their waterfront view.
Graham's son proposed the couple apologize or make a donation for coastal preservation, but they did not address this suggestion.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: KC Concepcion and Omar Cooper are standout wide receivers in the 2026 NFL Draft, showcasing route-running and speed.
Eli Stowers, a tight end from Vanderbilt, broke combine records, making him a strong candidate for Patriots' draft consideration.
Edge rushers Dani Dennis-Sutton and Malachi Lawrence showed exceptional athletic scores, placing them among top prospects for New England.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots unlikely to trade major draft picks for star players this offseason, per ESPN's Mike Reiss.
Patriots interested in Eagles' A.J. Brown and Raiders' Maxx Crosby but face high trade demands.
Coach Mike Vrabel emphasizes building through the draft; Patriots have 11 picks in 2026 NFL Draft.

title: Largest energy storage facility statewide gets approved for construction in Everett
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jupiter Power received state approval to build Massachusetts' largest battery energy storage facility in Everett.
The facility will span 16.5 acres at the former ExxonMobil site, featuring lithium-ion batteries and substations.
This project is the first phase of a development plan called 'Docklands,' led by The Davis Companies and Everett Landco.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Connor Storrie hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Connor Storrie hosted 'SNL' on Feb. 28, accompanied by his 'Heated Rivalry' co-star Hudson Williams.
Members of the US Olympic hockey teams appeared on 'SNL,' including Megan Keller and Hilary Knight.
Mumford & Sons performed as the musical guest on the 13th episode of SNL's 51st season.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s loss against the Red Bulls
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution lost 1-0 to the New York Red Bulls at Sports Illustrated Stadium.
Revolution center-backs Brayan Ceballos and Mamadou Fofana strengthened the defense despite the team's back-to-back season losses.
The Red Bulls' intense press limited the Revolution's effective passing and offensive opportunities throughout the match.

title: Triston Casas reportedly unlikely to be ready by Opening Day, while Kutter Crawford, Patrick Sandoval take step forward in rehab
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Triston Casas is unlikely to be ready for Opening Day after recovering from a torn patellar tendon.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval recently pitched live batting practice, marking progress in their injury recoveries.
Red Sox have an unclear roster for Casas, Crawford, and Sandoval due to off-season acquisitions and competition.
